'''Joshua Ralph''' (born 21 July 1997)<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sartorel |first=Thomas |date=2022-12-02 |title=Josh RALPH signs at Toulouse |url=https://www.to13.com/presse/josh-ralph-signs-at-toulouse/ |access-date=2024-04-15 |website=Toulouse Olympique XIII |language=fr-FR}}</ref> is a {{rlnt|Wales}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

==Background==
He was born on the [[Gold Coast, Queensland]], Australia.<ref name="MEET THE TEAM: Josh Ralph">{{cite web|url=http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16144/meet-the-team-josh-ralph|title=MEET THE TEAM: Josh Ralph|publisher=Wales Rugby League|date=27 July 2017|accessdate=15 November 2017}}</ref>

Ralph played for junior clubs [[Runaway Bay Seagulls]] and [[Nerang Roosters]].<ref name=meet>{{cite web|url=http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16144/meet-the-team-josh-ralph|title=MEET THE TEAM: Josh Ralph|publisher=|accessdate=14 November 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
He represented the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-15s before playing two seasons for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/playerstats/playerprofile/tabid/10898/clubid/116/playerid/7660/seasonid/44/default.aspx|title=Josh Ralph - Gold Coast Titans (U20) - NRL.com|website=www.nrl.com|accessdate=14 November 2017}}</ref> He then represented the Queensland under-18s before signing with the [[Newcastle Knights]] for the [[2017 NRL Under-20s season|2017 Holden Cup (Under-20s)]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/playerstats/playerprofile/tabid/10898/clubid/107/playerid/7660/seasonid/45/default.aspx|title=Josh Ralph - Newcastle Knights (U20) - NRL.com|website=www.nrl.com|accessdate=14 November 2017}}</ref>

Near the end of the 2017 season he rejoined the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref name=meet/><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16237/ralph-im-overwhelmed-and-humbled-to-be-here|title=Ralph: "I|publisher=|accessdate=14 November 2017}}</ref>

He was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Welsh squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]], qualifying through his Welsh grandmother.<ref name=meet/>

Ralph was also named in the Wales squad for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wales Men’s Squad Named For World Cup |url=https://wrl.wales/wales-mens-squad-named-for-world-cup |publisher=Wales Rugby League |access-date=28 October 2022 |date=3 October 2022}}</ref>

In December 2022, Ralph signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-make-wales-half-back-their-fifth-new-signing-for-2023 |title=Toulouse make Wales half-back their fifth new signing for 2023 |website=Love Rugby League |date=2 December 2022 |access-date=25 September 2024}}</ref> Toulouse reached the Grand Final, but lost to London Broncos thereby missing out on promotion to the Super League.<ref name=LRL22may24>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/8-familiar-faces-you-probably-had-no-idea-were-plying-their-trade-in-new-south-wales-cup |title=8 familiar faces you (probably) had no idea were plying their trade in New South Wales Cup |website=Love Rugby League |date= |access-date=25 September 2024}}</ref>

In the 2024 season, Ralph joined [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] playing for their [[NSW Cup]] team.<ref name=LRL22may24/>
